response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling experience — including work on NI LabWindows/CVI (a C IDE) and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack for this role is missing. There is no Kotlin or Spring experience at all, Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, internal tooling), and recent work centers on TypeScript/Node and DLang rather than JVM ecosystems. The tooling interest is real, but the required deep JVM, Java/Kotlin, and enterprise-framework expertise is not demonstrated.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong developer-tooling and testing background (test runner, assertion library, TDD/CI practices)
Broad polyglot experience across web, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput systems
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise-framework experience, both core requirements
Java exposure is minimal and dated, with no evident deep JVM expertise
Recent focus on TypeScript/Node and DLang, far from the enterprise JVM ecosystem
